claims.evidence_text: Ppd-D1a is the most potent known PI allele and the dominant source of PI in European and Asian cultivars... Ppd-D1a was the most potent known allele affecting the timing of flowering, requiring the least thermal time to flowering
claims.claim_summary: Wheat flowering time is strongly controlled by photoperiod sensitivity; Ppd-D1a confers the strongest photoperiod insensitivity and earliest flowering.

claims.evidence_text: All PI lines flowered earlier than Ppd-1b , with Ppd-D1a the most potent allele to induce flowering at the lowest mean thermal time of 1281 degree days ( s.e.d. = 15.51
claims.claim_summary: Photoperiod-insensitive (Ppd-D1a) spring wheat reached flowering at ~1281 degree-days of thermal time from sowing.

claims.evidence_text: Pots filled with the planting medium were soaked with water for 24 h prior to sowing with single seeds to a depth of 2.5 cm.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat seeds sown to a depth of 2.5 cm in pot experiments.

claims.evidence_text: Plots (minimum 2 x 5 m) were drilled (300 seeds/m 2 ) into a free-draining sandy loam
claims.claim_summary: Field wheat plots drilled at 300 seeds per m2.
